skill.name: youtube-comments-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users extract structured video list data and comment data from YouTube using the BrowserAct API. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when users request searching for YouTube videos and their comments, analyzing viewer sentiment for a specific video topic, gathering audience feedback on AI or automation, extracting a list of top videos and their viewer reactions, compiling YouTube video data along with user opinions, retrieving competitor video titles and related audience discussions, monitoring public response to specific YouTube search keywords, summarizing comments from search results for market research, tracking viewer engagement metrics and replies for trending topics, collecting YouTube video URLs and author details alongside community discussions, or automating the extraction of YouTube comments without manual scraping.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video metadata and comments via BrowserAct API for market research and audience analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. Network access targets only the intended BrowserAct API endpoint (https://api.browseract.com). Environment access reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY for authentication with proper credential handling. External command patterns in SKILL.md are markdown code blocks showing invocation examples, not actual execution. No malicious intent or data exfiltration detected. This is a legitimate YouTube data extraction tool.

low_findings.title: Network Access to BrowserAct API
low_findings.description: HTTP requests made to api.browseract.com - legitimate API usage for YouTube data extraction. No external exfiltration or malicious endpoints detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: API client makes requests to intended endpoint only - standard pattern for third-party service integration

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL for BrowserAct Service
low_findings.description: API_BASE_URL constant points to legitimate BrowserAct API - not suspicious, expected for API clients
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded API endpoint is standard practice for service-oriented clients

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Read for API Key
low_findings.description: BROWSERACT_API_KEY read from environment - legitimate credential management pattern, not a vulnerability
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard credential handling - key is stored in env var and sent only to intended API service

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Video Data and Comments
content.value_statement: Manually scraping YouTube for video data and comments is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates YouTube data extraction using the BrowserAct API, providing structured video metadata and comments without CAPTCHA or IP restrictions.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube by keywords and extract video metadata (title, URL, views, published date)
Retrieve comments from videos including author name, text, likes, and reply counts
Configure comment extraction limits and scroll counts for pagination
Monitor task execution status with timestamped logging
Handle API authentication via environment variables (BROWSERACT_API_KEY)
Extract data from multiple videos in a single operation
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key for authentication
Rate limits and quota depend on BrowserAct subscription plan
Cannot access private, unlisted, or age-restricted video comments
Network-dependent - requires internet connectivity to BrowserAct API

use_cases.title: Market Research and Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Gather video metadata and comments from competitor channels to understand audience preferences and market trends in your industry.

use_cases.title: Sentiment Analysis for Content Strategy
use_cases.description: Extract comments from videos about specific topics to analyze viewer sentiment and identify content opportunities based on audience feedback.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Automated Data Collection for Reports
use_cases.description: Collect YouTube video data and comments on trending topics to build datasets for business intelligence and research reports.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Comments for a Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the youtube-comments-api-skill to extract video data and comments for the keyword "{topic}". Get 20 comments per video and scroll 3 times. Provide a summary of the comments.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Competitor Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the youtube-comments-api-skill, search for videos from "{competitor_channel}" and extract comments to understand what viewers are discussing. Limit to 15 comments per video.

prompt_templates.title: Track Trending Topic Engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the youtube-comments-api-skill to extract comments about "{trend_topic}". Show me engagement metrics (likes, replies) and identify the most active discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Data Collection for Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the youtube-comments-api-skill to collect video and comment data for "{research_topic}". Extract 50 comments per video across 5 scroll iterations. Format the output as a structured dataset.

output_examples.input: Extract YouTube comments for 'AI automation tools'
output_examples.output: Found 5 videos matching your search. Extracted 100 comments total. Top video: 'AI Automation in 2024' with 45 comments. Most discussed topic: workflow automation benefits. Average comment sentiment: positive (78%).

output_examples.input: Get competitor video feedback for 'product review'
output_examples.output: Videos analyzed: 3. Total comments extracted: 60. Key feedback themes: pricing concerns (23%), feature requests (18%), ease of use (31%). Most engaged comment: 'Wishes it had more integration options' with 28 replies.

best_practices: Always set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running extraction tasks
Start with lower comment limits and scroll counts to test results, then scale up
Monitor the terminal output for status updates during long-running extraction tasks
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts or configuration files
Do not ignore 'Invalid authorization' errors - they indicate expired or invalid keys
Do not set excessively high comment limits without considering API rate limits

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations and generate an API key from your BrowserAct dashboard.

faq.question: What parameters does the skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts three parameters: keywords (search term), comments_limit (number of comments per video), and scroll_count (how many times to scroll for more comments).

faq.question: Why am I getting 'Invalid authorization' errors?
faq.answer: This error means your BrowserAct API key is invalid or expired. Check your BrowserAct dashboard for a valid key and ensure BROWSERACT_API_KEY is set correctly.

faq.question: How many comments can I extract per video?
faq.answer: The maximum depends on your BrowserAct subscription. Start with 10-20 comments per video and adjust based on your rate limit quota.

faq.question: What happens if the extraction times out?
faq.answer: The script has a 5-minute polling timeout. If the task exceeds this time, it will return an error. You can retry once, but if it continues to timeout, check your BrowserAct dashboard for task status.

faq.question: Can this skill bypass YouTube CAPTCHA or geo-restrictions?
faq.answer: Yes. The BrowserAct service handles CAPTCHA and IP restrictions, so you can extract data without dealing with these challenges.
